title: attributes — PHP attribute contracts
description: One class-level attribute discovered at boot. `AttributableEntity` binds a host model to the (entity_type, discriminator, values_column) triple so the AttributableEntityRegistry can resolve the active AttributeSet at runtime.

attributes.name: AttributableEntity
attributes.description: Marks a model class as an AttributeSet host. Boot-time compiler adds it to `AttributableEntityRegistry` so runtime resolution is a hash-map lookup.

params.name: discriminator
params.description: Column name on the host carrying the discriminator (typically `sport_key`).

params.name: valuesColumn
params.description: JSONB column name where attribute values persist. Defaults to `attributes`; hosts may override (e.g. `values`, `metrics`).

params.name: setResolver
params.description: Optional FQCN of a custom `AttributeSetResolver` implementation for the host. Defaults to the registry-driven resolver.

attributes.example:
#[AttributableEntity(entityType: 'athlete_enrollment', discriminator: 'sport_key', valuesColumn: 'attributes')]
final class AthleteEnrollment extends Model { use HasAttributeSet; }

output_registries: AttributableEntityRegistry — map of model FQCN → {entityType, discriminator, valuesColumn, setResolver}
discovery.cache: Redis, keyed on `attributes:registry:<sha256-of-scan>`. Invalidated on `artisan attributes:describe --refresh`.
